vue.answer: ## Building a Tag / Chip Input in Vue 3 **Mental model:** A tag input is an array of strings shown as chips plus a text field. Enter or comma commits the draft as a chip; Backspace on an empty field removes the last one. Trim and dedupe on commit. Keep it controlled with `v-model` so the parent owns the tags. ### Step 1 — Controlled tags + local draft ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Commit on Enter / comma ```js function commit() { const t = draft.value.trim(); if (t && !props.modelValue.includes(t)) emit('update:modelValue', [...props.modelValue, t]); draft.value = ''; } ``` ### Step 3 — Backspace removes the last chip Use `@keydown.enter` / `@keydown.backspace` modifiers; guard the empty-draft case. ```vue ``` ### Step 4 — Render chips ```vue {{ tag }} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Duplicate tag | `includes` check skips it. | | Whitespace-only | Trimmed on commit. | | Backspace mid-text | Only removes when the draft is empty. | **Interview tip:** Vue's key modifiers (`@keydown.enter.prevent`, `@keydown.backspace`) make the handlers declarative — using them instead of inspecting `event.key` shows fluency with the template event syntax.

angular.answer: ## Building a Tag / Chip Input in Angular **Mental model:** A tag input is an array of strings shown as chips plus a text field. Enter or comma commits the draft; Backspace on an empty field removes the last chip. Trim and dedupe on commit. Controlled via `tags` in + `tagsChange` out (enables `[(tags)]`). Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them), no `FormsModule`. ### Step 1 — Input/output + draft ```typescript @Input() tags: string[] = []; @Output() tagsChange = new EventEmitter(); draft = ''; ``` ### Step 2 — Commit on Enter / comma ```typescript commit(): void { const t = this.draft.trim(); if (t && this.tags.indexOf(t) === -1) { this.tagsChange.emit([...this.tags, t]); } this.draft = ''; } ``` ### Step 3 — Key handling Angular key-event filters call the right handler; Backspace checks the empty draft. ```html ``` ### Step 4 — Render chips ```html {{ tag }} ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | Duplicate tag | `indexOf === -1` check skips it. | | Whitespace-only | Trimmed on commit. | | Backspace mid-text | `onBackspace` only fires removal on empty draft. | **Interview tip:** Angular's `(keydown.enter)` event-filter syntax is cleaner than reading `event.key` — and naming the output `tagsChange` is what unlocks the `[(tags)]` two-way binding without `ngModel`.
